source_paper_title: Optimal Budget Aggregation with Star-Shaped Preference Domains
context:
Section 8 (Conclusion), page 19

Does Theorem 3 also hold when weakening group-strategyproofness to strategyproofness? Is continuity required for the characterization of the Nash product rule?
title: Characterization of Nash product rule without continuity (and with only strategyproofness) under Leontief utilities
latex:
Given integers \(n\ge 2\) and \(m\ge 2\), let \(\Delta^m:=\{q\in\mathbb{R}^m_{\ge 0}:\sum_{j=1}^m q_j=1\}\) be the simplex of budget distributions over alternatives \(M=[m]\). A profile is \(P=(p_1,\dots,p_n)\in (\Delta^m)^n\), where \(p_i\) is agent \(i\)'s peak (ideal distribution). Agents have Leontief utilities induced by their peaks: for each agent \(i\), let \(M_i:=\{j\in M: p_{i,j}>0\}\neq\emptyset\) and define
\[
 u_i^P(q)=\min_{j\in M_i}\frac{q_j}{p_{i,j}}\in[0,1].
\]
Define the Nash product rule \(\mathrm{NASH}:(\Delta^m)^n\to \Delta^m\) by
\[
\mathrm{NASH}(P)\in\arg\max_{q\in\Delta^m}\prod_{i=1}^n u_i^P(q),
\]
which is known to be single-valued.
A mechanism is any function \(f:(\Delta^m)^n\to\Delta^m\).
- \(f\) is strategyproof (SP) if for every agent \(i\), every profile \(P\), and every misreport \(p_i'\in\Delta^m\) (letting \(P'=(p_i',p_{-i})\)), we have \(u_i^P(f(P))\ge u_i^P(f(P'))\).
- \(f\) satisfies core fair share (CFS) if for every profile \(P\), there is no coalition \(S\subseteq [n]\) and distribution \(q'\in\Delta^m\) such that for all \(q''\in\Delta^m\) and all \(i\in S\),
\[
 u_i^P\Big(\tfrac{|S|}{n}q' + \big(1-\tfrac{|S|}{n}\big)q''\Big) > u_i^P(f(P)).
\]
Problem: Determine whether \(\mathrm{NASH}\) is the unique mechanism \(f\) satisfying CFS and SP on the Leontief domain, without assuming continuity and without strengthening SP to group-strategyproofness. Equivalently, is every (possibly discontinuous) strategyproof and core-fair-share mechanism necessarily equal to \(\mathrm{NASH}\) on all profiles \(P\in(\Delta^m)^n\)?
open_problem_quote: Does Theorem 3 also hold when weakening group-strategyproofness to strategyproofness? Is continuity required for the characterization of the Nash product rule?
